code_scope.file: N/A - EIP-7951 is Execution Layer (EL) feature
code_scope.function: N/A - Not implemented in Consensus Layer
code_scope.notes: Nimbus is a Consensus Layer (CL) client. EIP-7951 secp256r1 precompile is an Execution Layer (EL) feature implemented in clients like Geth, not in CL clients.

summary: EIP-7951 secp256r1 precompile is an Execution Layer feature not present in Consensus Layer client Nimbus.

phase0_scope_analysis.summary: Identified that EIP-7951 is an EL precompile (address 0x100) feature. Nimbus is a CL client written in Nim. Comprehensive search found no secp256r1, p256, or precompile implementation code.
findings: Only secp256k1 found (for P2P networking, not signature verification precompiles)
Execution Layer interaction is via Engine API only (beacon_chain/el/)
EIP-7951 would be implemented in EL clients (Geth, Nethermind, Besu, Erigon)

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Skipped - Code scope does not exist in this codebase

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Skipped - Code scope does not exist in this codebase

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: Not applicable - EIP-7951 operates in Execution Layer, which Nimbus CL only interacts with via authenticated Engine API
phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: Consensus Layer clients do not implement EL precompiles. CL-EL communication is via Engine API (JWT authenticated). Precompile execution happens entirely within EL.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Not applicable - Code scope does not exist in this codebase

phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: This property should be audited against Execution Layer clients (Geth, Nethermind, Besu, Erigon), not Consensus Layer clients
phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: EIP-7951 is part of the Fusaka hardfork and IS in-scope for the bug bounty, but must be audited in the correct client layer. Nimbus CL trusts the EL to correctly implement precompiles via Engine API.

recommendations: Re-assign this checklist item to an Execution Layer client audit (Geth, Nethermind, Besu, Erigon)
For Nimbus CL audit, focus on Consensus Layer features: PeerDAS (EIP-7594), validator operations, fork choice, blob handling
Update audit mapping to filter EL-specific EIPs when auditing CL clients
